notices.html: World Cup FINAL is Sunday: kickoff 3pm at MetLife Stadium. Heavy congestion is expected on Route 3, Route 120 and the NJ Turnpike near the Meadowlands starting a few hours before kickoff, so avoid that direction Sunday afternoon if you can.

Terhune's first-ever Flower Festival is this Saturday and Sunday, book timed tickets online ahead. Holland Ridge's fall Sunflower Festival (Sept 10 to Oct 12) is $17 per person; tickets typically go on sale in late summer, check hollandridgefarms.com.

blurbs.terhune: This weekend: the farm is centered on its first-ever Flower Festival Saturday and Sunday, book timed tickets online. Blueberries have only been picking Tuesday to Friday as supply allows, so call 609-924-2310 before counting on them this weekend. The spring freeze wiped out peaches and cherries. Store and donuts open 9 to 6, no pets.
blurbs.vonthun: This weekend: last weekend's Blueberry Festival has wrapped, but regular pick-your-own blueberries should continue at the South Brunswick fields, open 10 to 5 daily, field admission $3pp plus your container. Call 732-329-8656 to confirm before driving over.
blurbs.hickory: This weekend: no specific 2026 crop update found online for this small, diversified farm. Mid-July usually means early peaches, cherries, and summer vegetables, plus a $2 membership and $5 picking deposit per adult. Call 609-448-0629 for the day's pick. Open 9 to 6, Sundays 2 to 6.
blurbs.hollandridge: Nothing to pick this weekend. The fall Sunflower Festival runs September 10 to October 12, $17 general admission, 50 cents to $1 per stem. Tickets typically go on sale in late summer, check hollandridgefarms.com before counting on a date.
blurbs.locusthall: This weekend: blackberries ($8/qt) and early peaches ($10 quarter peck, $15 half peck) are self-serve picking Thursday to Sunday, 7am to 7pm, no entrance fee. Follow the signs to the fields and pay by QR code or cash on your way out.
blurbs.johnsonscorner: This weekend: blueberries are still picking (their season runs mid-June through July), plus blackberries, new this year. Hayrides, animals and soft-serve on site, open 8am to 7pm daily. Picking dates can shift a week or more, so call 609-654-8643 to confirm.
blurbs.battleview: This weekend: the spring freeze wiped out cherries, peaches, nectarines and apples this year, so no stone-fruit picking. The store sells corn and tomatoes, though pick-your-own status for those wasn't confirmed online. Call 732-462-0756 first.
blurbs.norzhill: This weekend: no confirmed u-pick crop yet. Their Sunflower Path (weekends 10:15 to 3:15, $10pp, kids under 36in free, stems 50 cents to a dollar) may not be open until later in the summer. Call 908-371-2697 to confirm before heading over.
blurbs.alstede: This weekend: their Very Berry Festival has wrapped, but blueberries and other berries should still be pick-your-own at regular rates most days. A 60-minute drive, so check their daily picking list or call before heading out.
blurbs.howell: This Saturday: Evening Animal Chores, starting 5pm, free. Help out as the animals settle in for the night. Closed Sunday. Check howellfarm.org/calendar to confirm before you go.

events.title: Flower Festival at Terhune Orchards
events.desc: New this year: pony rides, bubble parties, a magic show and pedal tractors amid the flower fields

events.title: LuminoCity Dino Safari (70+ light-up dinosaurs)
events.desc: 70+ glowing dinosaurs light up an outdoor trail after dark

events.title: Puerto Rican Parade and Festival
events.desc: Parade, floats, marching bands and dance groups on the Elizabeth waterfront

events.title: Mercer County Summer Concert Series

events.desc: Lawn concert at the Festival Grounds, bring chairs
